rationale: The changed detail was a set of employer names (re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective vs Naver/Tencent/MercadoLibre), yet neither evaluation's reasoning keys off the employer identity at all. Both versions reach the same substantive conclusion via the same logic: strong GIS/distributed-systems background, but C++ experience dated to 2014-2015 and no Rust, making the candidate a borderline fit for a C++/Rust role. The strengths and concerns are the same points reworded, and the 1-point score difference (5 vs 6) is within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing credits the famous Western/Asian employer names or penalizes the smaller ones.
